todayLessons.reason: Closing BAC at $52.02 for -2.7% loss ($163). Original thesis was 'oversold bounce with SPY +2.5% risk-on rotation' but BAC showing ZERO bounce after 1.5 hours - just continued weakness from $53.47 entry to $52.02. IASM not even providing signal on BAC (not in top opportunities). With SPY +2.3%, if financials aren't catching a bid in THIS environment, the thesis is broken. Cut loss and redeploy capital to better setup. Market giving us MU consolidation and other opportunities - no need to hold dead weight.

watchFor: AVOID: Trading in NEUTRAL regime with elevated VIX - 3 of last 4 days were NEUTRAL/choppy, all red days. You need RISK_ON directional moves
AVOID: Holding losers too long: BAC bled for 90 minutes with broken thesis before -2.76% cut. Your winners (AMD) are trimmed quickly, losers are given rope
AVOID: Forcing trades: 5 trades today in 'midday lull' when agent said wait for 2pm momentum. CRDO fade and BAC hold were both low-conviction
AVOID: Fading strength in wrong regime: CRDO +27% earnings gap fade lost -32%. In choppy NEUTRAL tape, don't fight momentum plays
tomorrowRules: NO TRADING if regime is NEUTRAL or VIX >18 - Your stats prove it: RISK_ON days = profit (Feb 6 +$20k, Feb 9 +$3.9k), NEUTRAL days = losses (Feb 10 -$2.8k, today -$6.2k). Wait for directional regime confirmation before first trade.

30-minute thesis checkpoint - If thesis isn't working within 30 minutes (like BAC showing 'ZERO bounce' in SPY +2.3%), cut immediately. Don't wait 90 minutes hoping for reversal. Max hold for broken thesis: 30 minutes.

Only trade setups with IASM confidence >65% AND clear catalyst - AMD (oversold RSI 7 + IASM 64%) barely qualified. CRDO fade had NO IASM signal. BAC had broken thesis. Require both technical + catalyst alignment.

Limit to 2 trades per day until win rate >50% - You're at 34.6% over 26 trades. This is systematic overtrading. Force selectivity by capping entries. Exception: Can add 3rd trade only if first 2 are both winners.

No fading earnings gaps in NEUTRAL regime - CRDO +27% gap fade lost -32% because you fought momentum in choppy tape. In NEUTRAL/elevated VIX, only trade WITH strong moves (like MU continuation), never against them.

dailyLessons.gradingReason: 34.6% win rate over 26 trades with 3-day red streak shows systematic execution problems. Today: -$6,207 (-6.7%) on NEUTRAL regime with elevated VIX 20, yet you traded 5 times into choppy tape. The AMD trim discipline was excellent (locked in +21.5%), but CRDO fade attempt and BAC loss show you're forcing trades in wrong conditions.
keyLessons: STOP TRADING IN NEUTRAL/ELEVATED VIX - You lost -$6,207 today in NEUTRAL regime with VIX 20. Your agent correctly identified 'midday lull phase - quality over quantity' at 11:51 yet you still entered CRDO fade and held losing BAC. Pattern: Feb 10 was NEUTRAL/-$2,870, Feb 11 RISK_ON/flat. You make money in directional regimes (Feb 6 RISK_ON/+$20,632, Feb 9 +$3,980), not choppy ones.

BAC thesis breakdown shows lack of conviction management - You held BAC for 1.5 hours watching it bleed from $53.47 to $52.02 with 'ZERO bounce' in SPY +2.3% environment. Your own lesson states 'if financials aren't catching a bid in THIS environment, the thesis is broken' - yet you waited 90 minutes to cut -2.76%. Rule: If thesis invalidates within first 30 minutes (no bounce in risk-on tape), cut immediately. Don't hope.

CRDO fade was forced trade into strength - Stock at $124 (-0.8% from entry) 'refusing to break lower' after +27% earnings gap means your bearish fade thesis never had edge. You lost -32% fighting momentum. Pattern: This mirrors your losing streak behavior - trying to catch reversals instead of riding trends. In NEUTRAL regime with choppy tech (AMZN -11%, NFLX -6.5%), fading strong semiconductor earnings (CRDO +27%, MU +9%) is low probability.

AMD trim execution was textbook - but highlights win rate problem: You captured +21.5% on AMD calls with perfect trim discipline (half at +18%, raised stops). This shows you CAN execute winners. But 9 wins vs 17 losses (34.6% win rate) means you're entering too many low-probability setups. The AMD oversold bounce (RSI 7, Z-score -2.64) had clear catalyst - most of your losses lack this conviction.

MU hold decision showed conviction: 'earnings beat catalyst intact, consolidation is healthy digestion not reversal' - proper thesis defense

dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Poor. -6.7% daily drawdown is excessive for account size. AMD trim showed good position sizing (took half at +18%), but overall trade selection was reckless: 5 trades in NEUTRAL regime when agent recommended waiting. BAC -2.76% loss after 90-minute bleed shows weak invalidation discipline. CRDO -32% fade into earnings strength was high-risk/low-edge. You're managing winners well but entering too many losers. Position sizing isn't the issue - trade selection is.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: Signs of frustration trading after 3-day red streak. Agent logs show correct 'NO_TRADE' call at 11:51 ('wait for 2pm momentum'), yet you entered CRDO fade at 12:02 anyway. BAC hold for 90 minutes despite 'ZERO bounce' shows hope/attachment over discipline. Pattern: You're not revenge trading (not sizing up), but you ARE forcing trades to 'get back to even' instead of waiting for A+ setups. The CRDO fade especially feels like trying to catch a quick win to offset the streak. Take tomorrow off if regime isn't RISK_ON - your stats prove you can't trade chop profitably.
